title: T-037 — GET /api/production/downtime-events with filters + analytics aggregations
prompt:
# T-037 — GET /downtime-events with filters

PRD: 08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §7.4 FR-08-E4-005, §8.2.5

## Goal
Implement GET /api/production/downtime-events?line_id=&date_from=&date_to=&category_id= returning paginated events plus aggregations (MTTR, top categories Pareto).

## Implementation contract
App Router GET. Query params line_id?, date_from?, date_to?, category_id?, page?, limit? (default 50, max 200). Returns {events:[...], pagination, aggregations:{mttr_minutes:number, total_downtime_min:number, top_categories:[{category_id, name, count, total_min, pct}], by_line:[{line_id, total_min, count}]}}. Aggregations computed inline via single SQL (window functions or CTEs). cacheLife({stale:60}).

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/app/api/production/downtime-events/route.ts [modify or merge with T-036]
- apps/web/app/api/production/downtime-events/__tests__/list.test.ts [create]
- packages/api-services/production/list-downtime-events.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given 50 events spanning 7d, when GET with date_from=7d-ago, then events array length matches and aggregations.mttr_minutes equals avg duration
2. Given top 3 categories cover 80% of total minutes, when GET, then aggregations.top_categories returns 3 rows with pct summing to ~80%
3. Given page=2 limit=10, when GET, then events length=10 starting at offset 10 (pagination correct)

## Test strategy
- RED: route test asserting events + aggregations + pagination before service exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test downtime-events.*list'

## Risk red lines
- Do not aggregate in JS (push to SQL for performance)
- Do not return >200 events per page
- Do not skip RLS

pipeline_inputs.description: GET /downtime-events list + Pareto + MTTR aggregations via single SQL.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single SQL CTE returns paginated rows + aggregations (MTTR avg duration, Pareto top categories with pct, by-line totals). Cache 60s. Max limit 200.
acceptance_criteria: Given 50 events 7d window, when GET, then events length matches and mttr_minutes equals avg duration
Given top 3 cover 80%, when GET, then top_categories.length=3 with pct summing ~80%
Given page=2 limit=10, when GET, then events length=10 starting offset=10
test_strategy: RED: route test asserting events + aggregations + pagination before service exists
